## [00:00 - 03:01]

Okay, so now I want to talk about scarcity with evergreen launches because this is one of the trickiest things is how do you deal with scarcity. As from going through product launch formula, as you know scarcity is one of the most powerful mental triggers there is, especially when it comes down to basically the launch, the opening cart, and especially the closing cart. At times these days we're seeing the final day of the launch of the cart open being bigger than the first day because of the scarcity. So scarcity is really, really powerful. So how do we do that if there's an evergreen launch? So generally the rule of thumb is evergreen launches are awesome. There's lots of cool things about them, but you do give up a little bit. And one of them I think is you give up a little bit in terms of scarcity in many markets. So I think the best way to deal with this is like, here's your, let's say this is your evergreen launch. And this is, first we have the squeeze page. Actually, let's go back to, here we go. You've got the squeeze page. And then they come in here and here's prelaunch content one and then two and then three. We go through here. And so if this person joining your list is Fred at Gmail or wherever, if they join your list here, then they run through your sequence and that sequence ends up on this sales letter. You open the cart, they go to the sales letter and then they've got, you know, whatever, three days until this is the cart close or the cart closes. So basically Fred, he goes through this prelaunch and now you give him this offer and you've got three days and he has three days to take advantage of whatever that special offer. And as we know from Product Launch Formula, you know, there's basically three primary ways to develop scarcity. One is the product goes off the market. The other one is the price increases and the third one is bonuses go away. Of course, you can do all of those or a couple of those, whatever you want. But those are the main ways of developing scarcity for your offer. Okay, so here's, you know, this is the cart close right here. And you have scarcity and you're emailing, you know, Fred joined your launch list and

## [03:01 - 06:04]

Fred basically has got these three days and let's just say the price is going up. Okay. So let's just say for this thing, you have, you know, if he takes advantage during this period of time, then he gets the thing for whatever, you know, for $97 instead of $147. That's the launch special. And maybe there's an extra bonus too. You know, maybe there's a bonus call or something like that. And so if he takes advantage of this, you know, he joins this, let's just say it's June 1st, he joins and the launch goes and the cart opens on June 8th and the cart closes on June 11th. Just making all this stuff up, right? That means he's got from June 8th to June 11th to go ahead and take advantage of this and get the special pricing and get the special bonus. This is no different from how the real world acts. You know, a lot of times, you know, if you sign up for a magazine subscription, you get special pricing or you can renew it special pricing. It doesn't matter when you sign up. It's just that offer is available when you come into the universe of that, you know, when you become a prospect. This is the same way. Now let's just say a week later, you know, Jean joins your list. Okay? So she's a week later, so that means that, you know, she's getting this offer on June 15th. The cart opens and whatever you say is going to be open for four days. So that means it closes on June 19th. Okay? That means Jean gets this deal, the 97 bucks and the bonus call, between June 15th and June 19th. Fred comes back. Fred comes back and he's going to get, he's going to either get, you know, it's not available, the car is closed or he's going to get this pricing because you set up special pages just for the next launch, just for, you know, the next time. You do, sort of like in that last video where I walk through, there's, you know, a launch each week. Here, you know, Fred gets, Fred's getting a launch one week, Jean's getting a launch the next week. Fred comes back. You're not going to give them the launch pricing or the launch bonuses. So that's one way, that's probably, in my opinion, the cleanest way to handle scarcity. It's based on when they came into your universe. Now, if Fred's like, it's really, really smart and he figures out what's going on, he might go and use a different email address and come through here and the next week and he's able to get your launch special the next week as well.

## [06:04 - 09:05]

It's not a perfect world. Those type of things can happen and depending on your market, they probably will. You know, if you're selling stuff into the knitting market, they're probably not going to figure this stuff out, but you know, if you're selling something into the internet marketing world, you know, a few of them are going to figure that out probably or they might just come across your site six months later. This is totally going to happen to you no matter what market you're in. They're going to come across your site six months later and they're going to join your list and they're going to go through that sequence again. If that happens, the reality is they're probably not even going to remember that they went through it the first time. People just are not as wrapped up in your material as you are. You know, the reality is a lot of people that join your list, they're not even going to follow through and get your pre-launch content. Sometimes people, you know, in June they might be not ready to see your content. They join your list again in December. Maybe they're ready to see your content at that time. That's just the reality. Not everyone who joins your list is going to go through your entire sequence. If someone goes through your sequence a few months apart, they're not going to remember the material. Trust me, I've got a lot of experience in this and for the most part, you know, one percent might actually remember having seen your stuff and even if they can remember having seen your stuff, they're not going to remember the pricing difference. So that's probably the best way to handle scarcity. I mean, another thing you can do if you're doing, say, the weekly launches or even especially like the monthly launch, you could limit the scarcity based on a number, on class size or quantity available. You're going to have to be a little more hands-on, but you can absolutely do that. Like for a couple of years, every time I rolled out product launch formula, I only allowed 500 people to come in because based on our customer service and our interaction, that was about the number that worked out well for us. So we cut it off at 500 each time. Well, you know, that was doing a launch every six months, eight months, every nine months. You could do the same thing if you're launching every single month. You could just say, this is the quantity we have. You know, John Gallagher, the food stamps to six figures guy, he did a monthly launch for years. I think he's probably still doing it for his herbal kits. These are physical kits and he has, he can only make up so many in a month and then each month he'll basically do a launch. The new people that came onto his list will get that sequence and they'll sell the kits, but there's only a limited number. So you can also limit number based on class size or quantity available or bonuses available. You can also do it limited based on class start. So maybe you're starting a new class every week or maybe you're starting a new class every month. That means they have to join in, you know, wherever between June 8th and June 11th or they're going to have to wait until the next class starts.

## [09:05 - 11:05]

That could work well if you're doing it like every month or every quarter. Another way you could do it is you can rotate offers. When they come into the world here, they make this offer. When they come into the world there, they make, they get a different offer or you could have pricing or whatever. So you can basically, you know, switch up your offers each week depending on when people come into your world. And then you could run people through this sequence and then you could maybe give them a break and then the people that joined for that launch, if they didn't buy, you can try them for this sequence because maybe they'll like that bonus. So there's a lot of mixing and matching that can be done throughout this whole thing with the scarcity. The number one thing with the scarcity, the way I approach it is they get, they get the deal based on when they come into your universe. Scarcity for Evergreen Launch.
